item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like test 3 and 4 are extratenous. You already know the thing is fuck ass huge and eats whales - putting the D boys in cages or trying to arm them just to test the creature strength seems cruel.

Still, I really like how they tested properly for the effects of similar liquids and the death of that poor D class going into the glass of water preventing them from doing more extended tests, so a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really really late but it’s literally necessary that it had been encountered before in our world. How do you think anyone had a description of its appearance to share in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first SCP to genuinely scare me. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, I really like incognito hazards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The cup just sucked him through? I wonder how that works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation can probably neutralize 90% of the objects in containment by shooting or blowing them up. If they did that they wouldn't be containing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good SCP! Interesting concept! I think I know how to kill this SCP though. To kill it, they could strap a waterproof bomb to a D-class. Then they could send the D-class into the water. When the monster eats the D-class, it will hopefully kill the monster with a huge explosion. That should eliminate the monster.. Then the foundation could study tissue samples from it. Unless the monster re-appears each time it's imagined. Which is very likely. But either way, it would be a cool experiment to try. Good SCP. Upvoted+.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one always reminds me of that episode of Courage the Cowardly Dog with the sea witch where the whole world is a thin skin over an endless sea.

… something was really wrong with that show

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just want to ask, but does the infected bring objects along with him? For instances, apparently the shark cage was transported, but how many objects could the subject bring along with him? What if the subject was in a submarine when he was in the water, would the submarine be transported as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I noticed i an earlier post someone stated that the experiment logs were too "repetitive." So, I came up with some experiment logs for you to try out:

1: Give half of the description to one test subject, and give the other half to another subject. Then, see if the effects work if they are both put in the same body of water at the same time.

2: See if animals other than humans (in this case, apes) are capable of SCP-1128 infection.

3: Show a false image of SCP-1128 to a test subject and see if someone can become infected if they believe they are (researching SCP-1128 on google images made me believe that I was infected.)

That's all I got at the moment but hopefully this will do. Enjoy.

item.title: I got pictures!
item.content:encoded:
lots of them - and I am so thirsty now….

Seriously though; I do wonder if in the SCP universe has anyone thought to write a story whereby there's a fragmented fossil of a mosasaur with notable bits missing and a much larger fossil of something was found nearby.
Also would fossilised remains have same effect as it being seen "in the flesh"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would be quite funny if a photo was added to this article just for April Fools, you know, just to give everyone a bit of a chill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is anybody reminded of the creature in Psychonauts that grabs you whenever you fall in a body of water?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one a lot but the one thing that irks me about it is that it's somewhere in our world that you end up teleported to. Seeing as how it draws you into some kind of wormhole to a vast ocean somewhere, it's highly likely this thing would have been encountered before in our world if it's big enough to devour whales in its jaws. What about removing the mobile task force and having the description pull you to either an alien ocean or a parallel dimension where the alternate earth is more flooded or has different oceanic life/variables.

Even with that, though, this is still one of my favorites. As a scuba diver, if I ever saw something that enormous and aggressive, I'd stay well away from the water too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favourite SCPs, despite I have a strong fear of large bodies of water and its inhabitants (also known as Thalassophobia). +1

item.title: Re: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded: I feel like I should bring the idea (if not draft) up in one of the forums, but I can't tell if Writing Help or Ideas and Brainstorming would be more appropriate.

item.title: Re: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
Not automagically, no. But people can change their votes if they don't like the new version and "if it ain't broke don't fix it". On the other hand it's your vision. It seems like a bold move regardless.

In Roget's history he mentions a popular article that swiftly nosedived after the original author made serious changes after leaving it alone for a long time. I believe they ended up reverting it to the popular version.

item.title: Re: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded: Well, maybe not necessarily rewrite so much as editing it in a few places- do upvotes go away when you do that?

item.title: Re: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded: Rewrite at +172? Bold.

item.title: Possible Rewrite
item.content:encoded: Just a heads-up, I have recently begun considering rewriting this one at some point. Especially since I have discovered what it was I had felt I failed to incorporate into this SCP in the first place…

item.title: Re: Could someone clear this up for me?
item.content:encoded: Depends how far along the "infection" is- at first it requires full submersion, but over time it'll take less and less water until theoretically you could step in a puddle and fall in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As said before, there is no canon when it comes to amnesiacs, it's up to the writer. It does complicate things, though.

item.title: Could someone clear this up for me?
item.content:encoded: Love this SCP, but its always confused me, does the bodily immersion requirement for getting teleported mean you have to be completely underneath the water's surface or would you be equally screwed if say, you were in the tub and just your legs were underneath the water.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are no oceans with 5-character names.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Interviews with these individuals carries some risk of further SCP-1128 contamination,"
Shouldn't this be "Interviews with these individuals carry…?"
+1ed for being awesome
About time, this is one of my favorites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When that D-Class mentioned huge teeth and it eating a whale, I thought of Gaghiel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adding images to articles is something only the author can do, since it's a stylistic edit. You can suggest pictures if you like, though I personally find this article just as good without one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Let's add a page image!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When this article was written that argument was alive and kicking (and I'm not so sure it's dead and buried these days), so it's not strange to see amnesiacs here1.

That said, if your goal was to get the word you prefer edited in, a far more effective way would be to contact the author and propose he do so.

Footnotes
1. Hell, I went from preferring amnesiacs to amnestics myself and only recently edited my older articles to match this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to say that the right word is amnestics, not amnesiacs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, my headcanon is that it's way out in the middle of the pacific, somewhere nobody really has any reason to go.

Or Austrailia.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, I like it. My only problem is:

Further investigation of these coordinates revealed the area to be [DATA EXPUNGED].

This doesn't even give the tiniest hint as to what's going on in the area that it lives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually don't remember where I got the idea of it existing in a real part of the ocean; I think I might have gotten that from the forums when I posted the idea for feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually like this a lot, great concept and you expanded upon it well.

Are you particularly attached to the idea of this creature's habitat actually existing? Considering how an encounter is provoked, I'd say an extradimensional ocean would suffice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was under the impression that class C were the weak specific-memory erasers while class B and A were progressively stronger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One thing that genuinely creeps me is the idea of large aquatic predators, so this gives me real chills.
Upvoted!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no canon when it comes to amnesiacs. When I write, personally, I use Class-C to refer to anterograde amnesiacs that we administer when we need someone to forget something that hasn't happened yet, but will, and Class-Omega is the total mind-wipe amnesiac.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only problem is talking about Class-C amnesiacs, everything else I've read describes them as being the amnesiacs that wipe your whole life, your personality everything. Class A's are short term from what I've read and that would be all that's required.
Other than that +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really digged the parts talking about repeated exposure. The SCP appearing in bodies of water was really nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Awesome! Reposted and double digits strong.

Wish I would have known it was up again earlier to give my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Keep trying! You've earned double digits; just resolve to use what you've learned here and make the next one better.

In other words, if lots of folks like it, don't worry about pleasing one person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-169 took that already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I half expected The Bloop to tie into this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Went ahead and changed the experiment log, and I'll get started on the containment procedures.

Also, should I bother expunging the bit about Kappa-12's duties, or can that bit stay?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Mentioned this over in the Experiment Log thread, but it's more relevant here: if not even dry land is safe, then your containment procedures are kinda lacking. You've created an apex predator for its environment (a section of the REDACTED ocean near the REDACTED coast). If this thing can eat whales, then it would be an apex predator on land by the REDACTED coast if it did in fact go on shore. In which case your containment procedures are lacking - Kappa 12 would need to be assigned to watch the land as well as the sea.

Also, regardless, I recommend putting mention of Kappa 12's duties in the containment procedures, since that's what they are - containment duties.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Satisfactory as-is, IMO. I was constantly hoping for any trace of a description besides "enormous undersea creature" while reading, but by the end I'd already thought up a dozen of them scarier than anyone could describe in words. That's how it's supposed to work. Nice job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kinda meant to play on the fear of enormous undersea creatures (something I tend to suffer from) as well as that "shark week" feeling of discomfort with being in/near water because it reminds you of some awful creature you saw in a documentary that lives in water (another thing I tend to experience). Still, I can't say I have the best writing skills on this wiki, so what can I do about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact that the thing is big enough to eat a whale (I have a definite fear of enormous undersea creatures), but this overall doesn't grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pulled into a glass of water? Excellent!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure if it was what you noticed, but I revised it a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First addendum has a mistake, but I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda true except for the last experiment (not even dry land is safe! MUAHAHAHAHA!!!), but I couldn't think of any other tests that could be done with this thing short of pitting 682 against it, and that probably would just get me negative ratings. Is there any way I could improve it that you can think of?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, the experiment log doesn't seem to be adding a lot to the article proper. It's pretty much the same point repeated over and over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ohh I like this one. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only issue - you don't have a title on the main listing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not a fan of the word 'massive' in 'massive aquatic predator' but you have my upvote again for the repost.
